When you are confident in the answer, emit the \boxed{} response.', started_at=datetime.datetime(2026, 4, 23, 2, 57, 39, 24317, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), finished_at=datetime.datetime(2026, 4, 23, 2, 57, 47, 102754, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), initial_observation='For which positive integers \( n \) does \( 5^{n-1} + 3^{n-1} \) divide \( 5^n + 3^n \)?', initial_info={'experiment_name': 'orz57k-tool', 'task_model_id': 'bedrock/google.gemma-3-4b-it', 'episode_index': 0}, steps=[TrajectoryStep(step_index=1, action="Let \( P(n) = 5^{n-1} + 3^{n-1} \) and \( Q(n) = 5^n + 3^n \). We want to find the positive integers \( n \) such that \( P(n) \) divides \( Q(n) \).

We can rewrite \( Q(n) \) as \( 5^n + 3^n = 5 \cdot 5^{n-1} + 3 \cdot 3^{n-1} \).
We can also rewrite \( P(n) \) as \( 5^{n-1} + 3^{n-1} \).

If \( n=1 \), then \( P(1) = 5^{1-1} + 3^{1-1} = 1+1 = 2 \) and \( Q(1) = 5^1 + 3^1 = 8 \). Since \( 2 \nmid 8 \), \( n=1 \) is not a solution.

If \( n=2 \), then \( P(2) = 5^{2-1} + 3^{2-1} = 5+3 = 8 \) and \( Q(2) = 5^2 + 3^2 = 25+9 = 34 \). Since \( 8 \nmid 34 \), \( n=2 \) is not a solution.

If \( n=3 \), then \( P(3) = 5^{3-1} + 3^{3-1} = 5^2 + 3^2 = 25+9 = 34 \) and \( Q(3) = 5^3 + 3^3 = 125+27 = 152 \). Since \( 34 \mid 152 \), we have \( 152 = 4 \cdot 34 + 16 \), so \( 34 \nmid 152 \).
However, \( 152 = 4 \cdot 34 + 16 \), so \( 34 \nmid 152 \). Let's check the division. \( 152 / 34 = 4.47 \dots \)
Let's redo the calculation. \( 5^2 + 3^2 = 25+9 = 34 \) and \( 5^3 + 3^3 = 125+27 = 152 \). \( 152 = 4 \cdot 38 \).
We have \( 152 = 4 \cdot 34 + 16 \), so \( 34 \nmid 152 \).

Let's try \( n=4 \). Then \( P(4) = 5^3 + 3^3 = 125+27 = 152 \) and \( Q(4) = 5^4 + 3^4 = 625+81 = 706 \).
\( 706 = 4 \cdot 152 + 98 \), so \( 152 \nmid 706 \).

Let's consider the case when \( n=5 \). Then \( P(5) = 5^4 + 3^4 = 625+81 = 706 \) and \( Q(5) = 5^5 + 3^5 = 3125+243 = 3368 \).
\( 3368 = 4 \cdot 706 + 544 \), so \( 706 \nmid 3368 \).

Let's consider the case when \( n=6 \). Then \( P(6) = 5^5 + 3^5 = 3125+243 = 3368 \) and \( Q(6) = 5^6 + 3^6 = 15625+729 = 16354 \).
\( 16354 = 4 \cdot 3368 + 3000 - 1344 = 16354 - 1344 = 15010 \).
\( 16354 = 4 \cdot 3368 + 16354 - 13472 = 4 \cdot 3368 + 2882 \).
So \( 3368 \nmid 16354 \).

If \( n=1 \), \( P(1) = 2, Q(1) = 8 \). \( 2 \nmid 8 \).
If \( n=2 \), \( P(2) = 8, Q(2) = 34 \). \( 8 \nmid 34 \).
If \( n=3 \), \( P(3) = 34, Q(3) = 152 \). \( 34 \nmid 152 \).
If \( n=4 \), \( P(4) = 152, Q(4) = 706 \). \( 152 \nmid 706 \).
If \( n=5 \), \( P(5) = 706, Q(5) = 3368 \). \( 706 \nmid 3368 \).
If \( n=6 \), \( P(6) = 3368, Q(6) = 16354 \). \( 3368 \nmid 16354 \).

Let \( n \to \infty \). Then \( 5^{n-1} + 3^{n-1} \approx 5^{n-1} \) and \( 5^n + 3^n \approx 5^n \).
We have \( \lim_{n \to \infty} \frac{5^{n-1}}{5^n} = 0 \) and \( \lim_{n \to \infty} \frac{3^{n-1}}{5^n} = 0 \).
So \( 5^{n-1} + 3^{n-1} \) will never divide \( 5^n + 3^n \).

Consider \( n=1 \). Then \( 5^{1-1} + 3^{1-1} = 1+1 = 2 \) and \( 5^1 + 3^1 = 8 \). \( 2 \nmid 8 \).
Consider \( n=2 \). Then \( 5^{2-1} + 3^{2-1} = 5+3 = 8 \) and \( 5^2 + 3^2 = 25+9 = 34 \). \( 8 \nmid 34 \).
Consider \( n=3 \). Then \( 5^{3-1} + 3^{3-1} = 25+9 = 34 \) and \( 5^3 + 3^3 = 125+27 = 152 \). \( 34 \nmid 152 \).

     {"tool": "python_exec", "arguments": {"code": "print(sum(i for i in range(1, 101) if i % 3 == 0))"}}
